data.title: Add recipe executor to CLAUDE.md quick reference and architecture section
data.description:
## Parent Story
b1cd-9bbd — Update project docs to reflect recipe infrastructure

## What to Do
Amend CLAUDE.md in two places:

### 1. Quick Reference Table (## Quick Reference section)
Add a new row after the existing ticket/sync rows:
| Run a recipe transform | `.claude/scripts/dso recipe-executor.sh <recipe-name> [--param key=value ...]` |

### 2. Architecture Section (## Architecture paragraph block)
Add a new bold-label sentence after the existing entries (before the agent routing table):
**Recipe registry and executor**: `recipes/recipe-registry.yaml` declares available recipes validated against `recipes/schemas/recipe-registry-schema.json` (JSON Schema Draft 7). CLI: `.claude/scripts/dso recipe-executor.sh <recipe-name> [--param key=value ...]`. Engine adapters live in `plugins/dso/scripts/recipe-adapters/`; supported engines: rope (Python AST), ts-morph (TypeScript AST), isort (Python imports), scaffold (file generation). Parameters are passed via RECIPE_PARAM_* env vars — never shell string interpolation. Transform recipes use git stash rollback on failure; generative recipes (`recipe_type: generative`) track and delete created files on failure.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Quick Reference table contains a row for running recipes with the shim CLI form
2. Architecture section contains a **Recipe registry and executor** bullet matching the above
3. No hardcoded absolute paths (portability check passes)
4. `check-referential-integrity.sh` passes (path refs resolve to real files — Layer 3 ensures scripts exist)
5. `plugins/dso/scripts/validate.sh --ci` passes

## Notes
- Do NOT use direct `plugins/dso/scripts/` paths in CLI examples — use the `.claude/scripts/dso` shim form
- The architecture description may reference actual file paths (e.g., `recipes/recipe-registry.yaml`) since those are data files, not subject to shim enforcement
